test_suite.details: 3327 passed, 1 failed. Failure: 'HTML is not stale (modified after or with wins.json)' — confirms build drift. All internal link, schema, and cross-reference tests passed.

internal_anchors.details: Test suite Section 3 (Internal Links) passed — all href targets and showTab calls verified by automated tests. No manual re-check needed.

tab_structure.details: 11 tab-content divs plus overview section. No duplicate tab IDs. All showTab targets resolve to valid tabs. Tab buttons reference: overview, evaluate, model, selftest, wins, pages, killshots, timestamp, predictions, falsify, ai, refs.

timeouts.note: Returns 403 to automated requests — likely bot protection, not broken. Resolves fine in browser.

external_links.details: Checked 12 gov/data source URLs and 7 other non-citation URLs. All resolved except noaa.gov which returns 403 to automated HEAD and GET requests (bot protection). NCEI links initially failed on HEAD but succeeded on GET retry. No broken links found.

mismatches: Build drift: 2272 substantive diff lines between workspace docs/index.html and fresh build from current data files. Changes include updated code_analysis counts (hardcoded: 25→26, live_fetch: 4→3), WIN detail text updates, and section prose updates from sections.json edits not yet rebuilt.
data_prose_consistency.details: The workspace HTML (dated April 15) is behind the current sections.json (modified April 15 20:43). A rebuild is needed. Date-only differences account for only 3 of 2275 total diff lines.

win_detail_consistency.details: 70 WINs in wins.json. All have tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict fields. WIN anchors use id='winNNN' format in HTML. Test suite Section 2 (HTML Output Consistency) and Section 5 (Data Cross-References) both passed, confirming WIN-level data integrity.

progressive_disclosure.details: All 70 WINs have tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict fields. Test suite Section 7 (Prose Content Integrity) passed. Section 8 confirmed 77 prediction panels checked.

issues.context: font-family: monospace; background: #f5f5f5; padding: 1rem — light background in code block styling
border-radius:6px;overflow:hidden;background:#fff — white background in patent link card

hardcoded_theme_colors.details: 2 hardcoded light-theme colors found in sections.json inline styles. Both are moderate severity — they produce white/light panels against the dark theme background.

discoverability.details: All files present: llms.txt (references 70 WINs, matches wins.json count), sitemap.xml (valid XML, contains main URL), robots.txt (Allow:/, Sitemap directive present). All meta tags present: description, og:title, og:description, ClaimReview ld+json. uncounted-failures.json: 8 entries, no duplicate IDs, all required fields present.

expansion_tracker.details: next_id is correct (no collision risk). 1 ID gap (EXP-052). 92 orphaned expansion files have no matching tracker entry — most are ISS-* prefixed investigation/research files and old EXP files from before the tracker was introduced. 5 phantom entries point to missing output files (EXP-020, EXP-021, EXP-022, EXP-027, EXP-065). No stale pending items.

workspace_only_files.details: 1 curmudgeon review file exists on workspace but not in git: WIN-027.c6.json. This is the latest review cycle for WIN-027 and is at risk of loss if the workspace is recycled. 0 analyst expansion files at risk.

workspace_sync_skips.details: No direction-violation skips in the last 24 hours. Workspace-sync boundary is being respected.

priority_queue_coupling.details: 0 coupled-category expansion items (section-new or category-proposal-writeup) that are not yet integrated. No decoupling issues.

curmudgeon_digest_coverage.details: 1 unprocessed review (WIN-027.c6.json) — within normal pipeline lag. Digest pipeline is processing reviews correctly. Note: digest reports 187 'under-covered' processed reviews with 1008 missing issues — this is the gap between curmudgeon findings and open-issues entries, which the decider addresses over time.

broken_review_files.details: Digest regenerated fresh. 0 parse errors in curmudgeon review files.

build_reproducibility.details: CRITICAL: 2272 substantive diff lines between published docs/index.html and fresh build from current wins.json + sections.json. Changes include: code_analysis count updates (hardcoded 25→26, live_fetch 4→3), WIN detail prose updates, section content updates. The site does not reflect the current source data. A 'node build.js publish' is required.

issues_found.description: Published docs/index.html has 2272 substantive differences from a fresh build. The HTML was last built April 15 but sections.json was updated at 20:43 UTC on April 15 without a rebuild. Code analysis counts, WIN detail text, and section prose are all stale in the published HTML.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Run 'node build.js publish' from the clean clone to rebuild and deploy.

issues_found.description: Curmudgeon review WIN-027.c6.json exists on workspace but not in git. This completed review is at risk of silent data loss if the workspace is recycled.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Commit WIN-027.c6.json to git via the clean clone.

issues_found.description: 92 files in monitor/analyst/expansions/ have no matching tracker entry. Most are ISS-* investigation files and older EXP files. While many may be intentional (pre-tracker era), this makes it impossible to know which represent unintegrated work.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Audit orphaned files — add tracker entries for any that contain unintegrated work, or move legacy files to an archive subdirectory.

issues_found.description: 5 tracker entries with status 'complete' point to output files that don't exist: EXP-020, EXP-021, EXP-022, EXP-027, EXP-065.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Either regenerate the missing output files or add no_output_file_reason to these tracker entries if the work was integrated by other means.

issues_found.description: 2 hardcoded light-theme colors in sections.json inline styles: #f5f5f5 background in part1.html (code block styling), #fff background in part6.html (patent link card). Both produce jarring white panels against the dark theme.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Replace #f5f5f5 with var(--card-bg) or a dark-theme equivalent. Replace #fff with var(--card-bg).

issues_found.description: ID gap in expansion tracker: EXP-052 is missing. This may indicate a deleted or renumbered entry.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Investigate whether EXP-052 was intentionally removed or lost due to a write collision.

issues_found.description: https://www.noaa.gov/ returns HTTP 403 to automated requests (bot protection). The link itself is not broken — it loads fine in a browser.
issues_found.suggested_fix: No action needed — this is expected behavior from NOAA's CDN. Monitor for persistent failures.

summary: FAIL: The most critical finding is build drift — the published HTML has 2272 substantive differences from a fresh build, meaning the site does not reflect the current source data (sections.json was updated after the last build). A 'node build.js publish' is required. One curmudgeon review (WIN-027.c6.json) is workspace-only and at risk of data loss. The expansion tracker has 92 orphaned files and 5 phantom entries pointing to missing output files. Two hardcoded light-theme colors in sections.json create dark-theme visual issues. All internal links, tab structure, discoverability infrastructure, and the digest pipeline are healthy. Test suite passes except for the staleness check confirming build drift.
